source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Backup Wrench Die Fall

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Backup Wrench Die Fall
actions_taken: All work on the drill floor was stopped
event.summary: On August 13, 2015, while performing drilling operations on Statoil's #002 well aboard the Transocean Discoverer Americas, a 2.8-pound Backup Wrench Die fell approximately 136 feet from the Modular Derrick Drilling Machine (MDDM) to the rig floor. The incident occurred when the Driller failed to disengage the Backup Wrench before hoisting the MDDM, causing excessive force on the die housing and resulting in the die becoming dislodged. No personnel were injured due to adherence to the Red Zone exclusion area during lifting operations.
recommendations: Implement formal training program for MDDM equipment operations
Establish supervisory verification of employee proficiency before equipment operation
Enhance operator awareness of warning indicators such as flashing display screens
Improve communication protocols between Driller and Toolpusher during equipment troubleshooting

threats.name: Failure to disengage Backup Wrench before hoisting
Operator failure to recognize warning indicators
Lack of supervisory oversight during equipment operation

consequences.name: Backup Wrench Die falls to rig floor
Potential personnel injury from dropped object
Equipment damage to MDDM die housing

controls.name: Red Zone Exclusion Area
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent personnel exposure to dropped objects
controls.lod_basis: Prevents injury consequence by keeping personnel out of hazard zone

supporting_text: No personnel were injured as a result of the incident due to everyone on the drill floor avoiding the 'Red Zone', an area that is off limits to the crew while lifting operations or being performed.

controls.name: Written Risk Assessment Review
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards before operations
controls.lod_basis: Intended to identify and mitigate hazards associated with drilling operations

supporting_text: Once complete, the drill crew held a meeting to discuss the upcoming operations and review the Written Risk Assessment to address the possible hazards associated with the job.

controls.name: Display Screen Warning Indicator (Flashing Light)
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to alert operator of system malfunction
controls.lod_basis: Intended to provide real-time feedback when Backup Wrench disengagement command fails

supporting_text: As the Driller pressed the command to disengage the Backup Wrench, the display screen started to 'Flash'. The flashing of the display screen should have served as a warning to the Driller that the command for the Backup Wrench to open was either not accepted or wasn't completed.
This light should have served as a warning to the Driller that the function was not accepted by the system or had failed to complete.

controls.name: Air Flex Brake System
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent unintended shaft rotation
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical safety system to block shaft rotation during certain operations

supporting_text: At this time, the Driller failed to notice a message that had been displayed on the top of the control panel stating 'Shaft Rotation Blocked by Air Flex Brake On'.
After four failed attempts at breaking the connection with no success, the Driller began to troubleshoot the issue and found that he had failed to disengage the Air Flex Brake.

controls.name: Supervisory Oversight and Guidance
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure operator proficiency and safe operations
controls.lod_basis: Supervisory verification of employee competency before equipment operation

supporting_text: Although the Toolpusher was the supervisor available to the Driller on the rig floor, the Driller never asked for any assistance with operating the equipment.

The investigation also showed that Transocean has no formal training program in place for the MDDM equipment and that the supervisory personnel onboard the Transocean Discoverer Americas failed to verify the Driller's proficiency before allowing him to operate the equipment.

Lack of Supervision: Failure of supervision onboard the rig to ensure that the employee was familiar with the equipment on the rig and a failure to properly train or give guidance to the new employee.

controls.name: Formal MDDM Equipment Training Program
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure operator competency
controls.lod_basis: Training ensures operators understand equipment operation and warning systems

supporting_text: Transocean currently has no specific training for employees on the operations of the MDDM system and software.
The investigation also showed that Transocean has no formal training program in place for the MDDM equipment

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
